original_reasoning: يبرين=Yabrin/Berea (town near Aleppo); Βέροιαν=Beroia/Berea. Direct place-name Semitic-Greek match

الشرنتي = a bird (طائر). λαλίστερος = more talkative/chattering (comparative of λάλος). Birds are the archetypal chatterers in every language. A bird named الشرنتي and an adjective meaning 'more chattering' meet perfectly at the bird-song/chatter intersection.

الآمِص = طعام يتّخذ من لحم عجل بجلده (a food prepared from calf meat with its skin). λαιμός = throat, gullet. Food and the throat are directly linked through the act of eating — food is what passes through the throat. The anatomical pathway of consumption connects them strongly.
Driving away creates separation or opening; gape/open wide is a plausible abstract drift.

الخضا: تفتّت الشيء الرطب / وانفضاخه (crumbling/breaking apart of moist matter). οὐλοχύται=barley grains poured over sacrificial victim. Both involve granular matter being broken/scattered. Crumbling moist substance and scattered grain share the particulate-dispersion concept.
Arabic going forth or roaming freely overlaps with Greek entering through the broader core of directed movement.
Arabic صبّ 'pour/make flow into' vs Greek 'caused to go into' (participle of εἰσβιβάζω); both involve directed flow/entry into
اصخات is the calming or subsiding of swelling and recovery, which plausibly meets σχέτε in the sense of holding or being in a settled state.
